question: For Clinical Study NCT04063163 titled 'A Phase III Study to Investigate Efficacy and Safety of HLX10 + Chemotherapy in Patients With ES-SCLC': What were the exact inclusion criteria for participants in this clinical trial, including age range, disease stage, and required biomarkers
ground_truth_answer: The inclusion criteria for participants in this clinical trial included patients with extensive-stage small cell lung cancer (ES-SCLC) who had not previously received systemic therapy. The exact age range and required biomarkers were not mentioned in these contexts.
source_chunks: Of 894 patients who were screened, 585 with extensive-stage SCLC who had not previously received systemic therapy were randomized.
Among the 585 patients who were randomized (mean age, 61.1 [SD, 8.67] years; 104 [17.8%] women)
Chunk 3: ClinicalTrials.gov Identifier: NCT04063163.

Chunk 4: This international, double-blind, phase 3 randomized clinical trial (ASTRUM-005) enrolled patients at 114 hospital sites in 6 countries between September 12, 2019, and April 27, 2021. Of 894 patients who were screened, 585 with extensive-stage SCLC who had not previously received systemic therapy were randomized. Patients were followed up through October 22, 2021.

Chunk 5: Patients were randomized 2:1 to receive either 4.5 mg/kg of serplulimab (n = 389) or placebo (n = 196) intravenously every 3 weeks. All patients received intravenous carboplatin and etoposide every 3 weeks for up to 12 weeks.

Chunk 6: The primary outcome was overall survival (prespecified significance threshold at the interim analysis, 2-sided P < .012). There were 13 secondary outcomes, including progression-free survival and adverse events.

Chunk 7: To evaluate the efficacy and adverse event profile of the PD-1 inhibitor serplulimab plus chemotherapy compared with placebo plus chemotherapy as first-line treatment in patients with extensive-stage SCLC.

question: For Clinical Study NCT04063163 titled 'A Phase III Study to Investigate Efficacy and Safety of HLX10 + Chemotherapy in Patients With ES-SCLC': Describe the trial design: Was it randomized, controlled, blinded (single/double), parallel or crossover, and what was the sample size per arm
ground_truth_answer: The trial was a randomized, double-blind, controlled, parallel study with a 2:1 allocation ratio. The sample size per arm was 389 patients in the serplulimab group and 196 patients in the placebo group.

question: For Clinical Study NCT04063163 titled 'A Phase III Study to Investigate Efficacy and Safety of HLX10 + Chemotherapy in Patients With ES-SCLC': What was the primary endpoint of this study, how was it measured (e.g., PFS at 12 months, response rate), and what was the predefined success threshold
ground_truth_answer: The primary endpoint of the study was overall survival (OS), measured as the time from randomization to death. The predefined success threshold was a two-sided P-value of less than 0.012 at the interim analysis.

question: For Clinical Study NCT04063163 titled 'A Phase III Study to Investigate Efficacy and Safety of HLX10 + Chemotherapy in Patients With ES-SCLC': What secondary endpoints were assessed, including any exploratory ones like quality-of-life scores or biomarker changes
ground_truth_answer: The secondary endpoints assessed in the study included progression-free survival, adverse events, and quality-of-life scores measured by EORTC QLQ-C30, QLQ-LC13, and EQ-5D-5L questionnaires. Improvements were noted in the 'pain in other parts' symptom domain for the serplulimab arm. Biomarker changes were also explored, though specific details were not provided in the available context.

Chunk 8: Among the 585 patients who were randomized (mean age, 61.1 [SD, 8.67] years; 104 [17.8%] women), 246 (42.1%) completed the trial and 465 (79.5%) discontinued study treatment. All patients received study treatment and were included in the primary analyses. As of the data cutoff (October 22, 2021) for this interim analysis, the median duration of follow-up was 12.3 months (range, 0.2-24.8 months). The median overall survival was significantly longer in the serplulimab group (15.4 months [95% CI, 13.3 months-not evaluable]) than in the placebo group (10.9 months [95% CI, 10.0-14.3 months]) (hazard ratio, 0.63 [95% CI, 0.49-0.82]; P < .001). The median progression-free survival (assessed by an independent radiology review committee) also was longer in the serplulimab group (5.7 months [95% CI, 5.5-6.9 months]) than in the placebo group (4.3 months [95% CI, 4.2-4.5 months]) (hazard ratio, 0.48 [95% CI, 0.38-0.59]). Treatment-related adverse events that were grade 3 or higher occurred in 129 patients (33.2%) in the serplulimab group and in 54 patients (27.6%) in the placebo group.

question: For Clinical Study NCT04063163 titled 'A Phase III Study to Investigate Efficacy and Safety of HLX10 + Chemotherapy in Patients With ES-SCLC': What were the primary endpoint results, including point estimates, confidence intervals, p-values, and whether the primary objective was met
ground_truth_answer: The primary endpoint of overall survival was met with a median OS of 15.4 months (95% CI, 13.3 months-not evaluable) in the serplulimab group versus 10.9 months (95% CI, 10.0-14.3 months) in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 0.63 [95% CI, 0.49-0.82]; P < .001). The prespecified significance threshold was P < .012, and the primary objective was met.

question: For Clinical Study NCT04063163 titled 'A Phase III Study to Investigate Efficacy and Safety of HLX10 + Chemotherapy in Patients With ES-SCLC': What statistical methods were used for the primary analysis (e.g., Kaplan-Meier, Cox regression, ITT vs. per-protocol), including multiplicity corrections
ground_truth_answer: The primary analysis for Clinical Study NCT04063163 utilized Kaplan-Meier estimates to determine survival medians and Cox regression to calculate hazard ratios. The interim analysis applied a two-sided significance threshold of P < .012. The study followed an intention-to-treat (ITT) approach, including all randomized patients in the analyses. Multiplicity correction was addressed through the prespecified significance threshold for the interim analysis.
